steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Text 2 responds to the criticism in Text 1. Text 2 defends Asimov by arguing his flat characters are a deliberate choice — individuals are meant to be unimportant given the sweep of history he depicts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — misrepresents Text 2's stance: Text 2 does not concede the criticism or shift the conversation away from characterization. ✗
  • Choice B — introduces an unrelated comparison: Text 2 does not compare Asimov to other authors to make its point. ✗
  • Choice C — contradicts Text 2's claim: Text 2 does not argue that Asimov's characters are actually well-developed. ✗
  • Choice D — minimal characterization is intentional and serves Asimov's thematic goal: This matches Text 2's argument that flat characters are a purposeful device to convey that individuals don't shape historical forces. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
